sourceMovie.title: 28 Days Later
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto 28 Days Later (2002) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: World War Z
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: World War Z (2013) is the kind of film 28 Days Later fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The horror overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: 28 Weeks Later
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: 28 Weeks Later shares 28 Days Later's gift for horror — the 2007 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: 28 Years Later
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes 28 Days Later compelling — the horror, the thriller — shows up in 28 Years Later (2025) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Long Walk
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror energy of 28 Days Later is alive in The Long Walk (2025), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) operates in the same thriller as 28 Days Later, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Twelve Monkeys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Twelve Monkeys (1995) belongs to the same conversation as 28 Days Later — both anchor their thriller in science-fiction emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Death Cure
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 28 Days Later's thriller stayed with you, Maze Runner: The Death Cure (2018) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The science-fiction through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Dawn of the Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where 28 Days Later excels in horror, Dawn of the Dead (1978) doubles down on science-fiction to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: [REC]²
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: [REC]² (2009) is the natural next step after 28 Days Later: same horror DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Girl with All the Gifts
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of 28 Days Later who respond to its horror will find The Girl with All the Gifts (2016) immediately familiar. Both films share a science-fiction sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

faq.question: What should I watch after 28 Days Later?
faq.answer: Try World War Z, 28 Weeks Later, 28 Years Later—each shares DNA with 28 Days Later while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is 28 Days Later?
faq.answer: 28 Days Later (2002) is best described as Horror, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is 28 Days Later part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "28 Days/Weeks/Years Later Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
